description_long: This town hall is a rectangular, single-story building with a prominent terracotta-tiled gabled roof and a smaller annex attached to one side. The main structure features large, arched windows with decorative trim, and the facade is accented by pilasters and subtle moldings. The walls are finished in a light, neutral color, contrasting with the darker roof and window frames. The annex has a simpler design, with smaller rectangular windows and a single door, suggesting a utilitarian function. The building sits on a flat, paved base, typical for civic structures in urban environments. No visible signage or flags are present, and the overall style is reminiscent of early 20th-century European civic architecture with restrained ornamentation.
description: A rectangular, single-story town hall with a terracotta-tiled gabled roof, arched windows, and a smaller annex. The facade features decorative trim and pilasters, with a light wall finish and no visible signage.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main facade with large arched windows, decorative trim, and the prominent gabled roof, as well as the attached annex on the left.
description_view_1: The side view shows the length of the building, the tiled roof, and the annex's simpler wall with smaller windows and a single door.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the annex's plain facade, the rear of the main building, and the continuous roofline.
description_view_3: The view displays the main entrance area with arched windows and pilasters, as well as the annex's side and the paved base.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Pilasters, arched window trim, subtle moldings
attribute_entrance_design: Single main entrance with arched windows, framed by pilasters and decorative trim, no steps or portico visible
